steps.action: Launched the native Tauri app on `/settings` with the isolated HOME, captured the window, and inspected the isolated SQLite database.
steps.expected: The Settings page should list built-in scan directories and any custom directories.
steps.observed: The captured Settings page visibly listed built-in scan directory rows labeled `内置目录`, and the isolated database contained 10 seeded built-in `scan_directories` rows matching the built-in agent paths.

assertions.title: Add custom scan directory

steps.action: Tried to delegate the rerun to a `user-testing-flow-validator` subagent, then manually targeted the visible `添加目录` button with OCR-mapped Quartz mouse clicks and captured the window again.
steps.expected: The Add Directory dialog should open so a custom path can be entered and rescanned.
steps.observed: The Task subagent again failed immediately with `insufficient permission to proceed`, the follow-up screenshot stayed unchanged, and the isolated `scan_directories` row count remained 10, so the Add Directory dialog never opened on the real user surface.

assertions.issues: Blocked because the native Tauri window remained non-interactive in this rerun: OCR-mapped CGEvent clicks on visible controls produced no UI change, `AXFrontmost` stayed false, and the accessibility tree never exposed actionable child elements.

assertions.title: Remove custom scan directory

steps.action: Attempted to establish any working input path in the native Settings window before exercising directory removal.
steps.expected: A removable custom directory row should be operable so removing it triggers a rescan.
steps.observed: No reliable input reached the native window in this rerun, so a removable custom-directory row could not be created or operated on the real user surface.

assertions.issues: Blocked because the native Tauri window remained non-interactive in this rerun: OCR-mapped CGEvent clicks on visible controls produced no UI change, `AXFrontmost` stayed false, and the accessibility tree never exposed actionable child elements.

assertions.title: Add custom platform

steps.action: Prepared an isolated custom-platform directory and then attempted to use the native Settings controls needed to register it.
steps.expected: The Add Platform dialog should open so a custom platform name and directory can be submitted.
steps.observed: The same native-window input blocker prevented opening the Add Platform control, so the custom-platform registration flow could not begin on the real user surface.

assertions.issues: Blocked because the native Tauri window remained non-interactive in this rerun: OCR-mapped CGEvent clicks on visible controls produced no UI change, `AXFrontmost` stayed false, and the accessibility tree never exposed actionable child elements.

assertions.title: Custom platform shows skills

steps.action: Prepared `platform-native-skill` in the isolated custom-platform directory but first needed to complete the Add Platform UI flow.
steps.expected: After adding a custom platform and rescanning, its platform view should display the native skill from that directory.
steps.observed: Because the Add Platform dialog could not be opened on the real user surface, the prerequisite platform registration never occurred and the downstream skill-visibility assertion could not be exercised.

assertions.issues: Blocked by VAL-SET-004 in this rerun: the native Add Platform flow could not be exercised on the real user surface.

assertions.title: Custom directory -> rescan -> visible

steps.action: Prepared the isolated custom-scan fixture directory and then tried to open the Add Directory control needed to rescan it.
steps.expected: A user should be able to add the directory in Settings and see the new skill appear after rescan.
steps.observed: The Add Directory interaction remained unreachable on the real user surface, so the end-to-end custom-directory journey could not be executed.

assertions.issues: Blocked by VAL-SET-002 in this rerun: the native Add Directory flow could not be exercised on the real user surface.

assertions.title: Custom platform -> install -> verify

steps.action: Prepared the isolated custom-platform directory and central skill fixtures, then attempted to begin the Settings add-platform step.
steps.expected: A user should be able to add a custom platform, install a central skill to it, and verify the skill appears in that platform view.
steps.observed: The add-platform step never became operable on the real user surface, so the downstream install-and-verify journey could not start.

assertions.issues: Blocked by VAL-SET-004 in this rerun: the native Add Platform flow could not be exercised on the real user surface.

assertions.title: Navigation consistency

steps.action: Confirmed the Settings route rendered correctly, then attempted to establish a working click path for sidebar/back navigation in the native window.
steps.expected: Every view should be reachable from the sidebar or navigation controls, and the sidebar should reflect the active view as a user navigates.
steps.observed: Because no reliable user input landed on the native Tauri window, actual sidebar/back-navigation interactions still could not be exercised in this rerun.

assertions.issues: Blocked because the native Tauri window remained non-interactive in this rerun: OCR-mapped CGEvent clicks on visible controls produced no UI change, `AXFrontmost` stayed false, and the accessibility tree never exposed actionable child elements.

assertions.title: Import collection from JSON

steps.action: Prepared a valid collection JSON fixture in the isolated Downloads directory and confirmed the Collections import control remained visible in the native sidebar.
steps.expected: A user should be able to activate the import control, choose the JSON file, and see the imported collection created.
steps.observed: Because the native window still would not accept actionable user input, the import control could not be activated on the real user surface in this rerun.

assertions.issues: Blocked because the native Tauri window remained non-interactive in this rerun: OCR-mapped CGEvent clicks on visible controls produced no UI change, `AXFrontmost` stayed false, and the accessibility tree never exposed actionable child elements.

frictions.description: Delegating the rerun to a `user-testing-flow-validator` subagent was still not possible in this worker session.
frictions.resolution: Recorded the immediate permission failure and continued the rerun manually inside the worker.

frictions.description: The native Tauri window rendered correctly, but OCR-mapped Quartz click attempts on visible controls produced no UI change and the AX tree still exposed only application/menu-bar nodes.
frictions.resolution: Captured screenshot + SQLite evidence for the passed assertion and treated interaction-dependent assertions as blocked.

blockers.description: Native Tauri input automation remains blocked in this rerun: `AXFrontmost` stayed false, the AX tree never exposed actionable children, and CGEvent clicks on visible controls were no-ops.
blockers.resolution: A future rerun needs a working foreground/input path for the native app window or another automation surface capable of driving the real Tauri UI.

summary: Reran the 9 remaining settings-polish assertions against an isolated native Tauri instance. VAL-SET-001 now passes because built-in scan directories are visibly rendered and seeded in SQLite, while the other 8 assertions remain blocked by the same native-window input automation issue that prevents dialogs, sidebar navigation, and the collection import control from being activated on the real user surface.
